content: How does 1 John 4:8 define God?
explanation: 1 John 4:8 - God is love. Whoever does not love does not know God.
options: God is fire
God is a spirit
God is love
God is light

content: If we confess our sins, what will the Lord do according to 1 John 1:9?
explanation: 1 John 1:9 - The Lord is faithful and just to forgive our sins and cleanse us from all unrighteousness. We only need to confess truly.
options: Require us to prove it
Punish us
Turn His face away
Forgive our sins and cleanse us from all unrighteousness

content: 1 John 1:7: If we walk in the light, what does the blood of Jesus do?
explanation: Walking in the light + the blood of the Lord = continual cleansing from sin.
options: Heals us
Cleanses us from all sin
Protects us
Comforts us

content: 1 John 2:1-2: When we sin, who is our Advocate?
explanation: Jesus is our Advocate and also the propitiation for our sins.
options: Peter
An angel
Jesus Christ, the Righteous One
Moses

content: 1 John 2:15: "Do not love ___" for all that is in it is not from the Father
explanation: The world's value system (lust of the flesh, lust of the eyes, pride of life) opposes God.
options: Friends
Family
Work
The world and the things in the world

content: 1 John 3:16: By this we know love: He ___ for us
explanation: The definition of love: laying down one's life. And we also ought to do the same for the brethren.
options: Laid down His life
Healed
Prayed
Taught

content: 1 John 3:18: "Let us love not only in word and tongue but in ___"
explanation: True love is expressed through concrete actions, not just words.
options: Deed and truth
Promises
Feelings
Money

content: 1 John 4:18: What does perfect love do with fear?
explanation: When we know God loves us perfectly, fear is driven away.
options: Hides fear
Is unrelated
Increases fear
Casts out fear

content: 1 John 4:19: "We love because ___ first loved us"
explanation: Our love is only a response to the love God gave first.
options: Of course
We want to
He (God)
We must

content: 1 John 5:3: What does it mean to love God?
explanation: Love = obedience. And God's commandments are not burdensome when the Holy Spirit helps.
options: Going to church
Keeping His commandments, and His commandments are not burdensome
Praying a lot
Giving offerings

content: 1 John 5:14: If we ask anything according to God's will, He will ___
explanation: Praying according to God's will is the key to being heard and answered by Him.
options: Wait
Hear us
Consider
Test

content: 1 John 4:10: This is love: not that we loved God but that ___ loved us and sent His Son as a propitiation for our sins
explanation: Love is initiated by God, not by us. God actively loved first.
options: A prophet
An angel
He
We

content: What senses does 1 John 1:1 say the apostles used to bear witness to the Word of life?
explanation: 1 John 1:1 — 'That which was from the beginning, which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes, which we looked upon and have touched with our hands, concerning the word of life.'
options: Heard only
Saw only in vision
Heard, seen, looked upon, and touched
Read only in Scripture

content: According to 1 John 1:5, what is the message Christ brought about God?
explanation: 1 John 1:5 — 'God is light, and in him is no darkness at all.'
options: Light, with no darkness in Him
A stern judge
Creator of all
Invisible One

content: What does 1 John 1:8 say about claiming to have no sin?
explanation: 1 John 1:8 — 'If we say we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.'
options: Will be blessed
We deceive ourselves and the truth is not in us
Will avoid punishment
Will become holy

content: How does 1 John 2:3-4 say we know we have come to know Him?
explanation: 1 John 2:3 — 'And by this we know that we have come to know him, if we keep his commandments.'
options: By spiritual feelings
By miracles
If we keep His commandments
By Bible knowledge

content: How does 1 John 2:22 define the antichrist?
explanation: 1 John 2:22 — 'Who is the liar but he who denies that Jesus is the Christ? This is the antichrist, he who denies the Father and the Son.'
options: The adulterer
The murderer
The one who leaves the church
The one who denies that Jesus is the Christ

content: What does 1 John 3:1 say we are called by the Father's love?
explanation: 1 John 3:1 — 'See what kind of love the Father has given to us, that we should be called children of God.'
options: Servants of God
Children of God
Friends of God
Messengers of God

content: What does 1 John 3:9 say about the one born of God?
explanation: 1 John 3:9 — 'No one born of God makes a practice of sinning, for God's seed abides in him; and he cannot keep on sinning, because he has been born of God.'
options: Still sins as before
Only sins minor sins
Is never sad
Does not make a practice of sinning

content: What does 1 John 5:11-12 say whoever has the Son has?
explanation: 1 John 5:12 — 'Whoever has the Son has life; whoever does not have the Son of God does not have life.'
options: Has life
Has power
Has wealth
Has wisdom

content: According to 1 John 4:16, what have believers KNOWN and BELIEVED? Who abides in love?
explanation: 1 John 4:16 — 'So we have come to know and to believe the love that God has for us. God is love, and whoever abides in love abides in God, and God abides in him.' SECOND time 'GOD IS LOVE' (4:8 first time). Core of 1 John. Does NOT say God HAS love — he IS love in his being.
options: Known God's strength — who lives well abides in him
Known God's righteousness — who does good abides in him
KNOWN + BELIEVED the love God has for us — God IS LOVE; whoever abides in love abides in God, and God abides in him
Known God's wisdom — who is wise abides in him

content: According to 1 John 1:3, what is John's purpose in PROCLAIMING to believers?
explanation: 1 John 1:3 — 'that which we have seen and heard we proclaim also to you, so that you too may have fellowship with us; and indeed our fellowship is with the Father and with his Son Jesus Christ.' 'FELLOWSHIP' (koinōnia) = sharing, communion. The Gospel does NOT just teach theory — it draws people into RELATIONSHIP with Father + Son + community.
options: So they learn doctrine (according to the text's framing)
So they may be healed (per the textual narrative)
So they may do miracles (as described in the passage)
So that they may also have FELLOWSHIP with us — and our fellowship is WITH the FATHER and his SON Jesus Christ

content: According to 1 John 1:6, if one CLAIMS fellowship with God yet WALKS in DARKNESS, what?
explanation: 1 John 1:6 — 'If we say we have fellowship with him while we walk in darkness, we lie and do not practice the truth.' 1 John tests FALSE faith with 3 'IF WE SAY': (1) 'I have fellowship' but walk in dark = lie; (2) 'No sin' (1:8); (3) 'Not sinned' (1:10). All WORDS contradict ACTIONS.
options: He LIES and does NOT practice the truth
Still receives God's mercy
Just a slow learner
Weak in faith (per the textual narrative)

content: According to 1 John 1:10, if one CLAIMS NEVER to have SINNED, what about God?
explanation: 1 John 1:10 — 'If we say we have not sinned, we make him a liar, and his word is not in us.' Contrasts 1:8 ('no sin') with 1:10 ('never sinned'). God has DECLARED all people sinners — denying = making GOD a LIAR. Extremely serious.
options: Honors him (per the textual narrative)
Praises him (according to the text's framing)
Makes him a LIAR — and his Word is NOT in us
Is humble before him

content: According to 1 John 2:6, whoever claims to abide in Christ must walk how?
explanation: 1 John 2:6 — 'whoever says he abides in him ought to walk in the same way in which he walked.' 'AS HE WALKED' = Christ's earthly life. The IMITATIO CHRISTI concept — imitate Christ. Saying 'I ABIDE IN HIM' is not enough — must WALK as HE walked. Faith is NOT a profession — it's a life.
options: Walk as a Jew (per the textual narrative)
Walk in the same way in which HE WALKED
Walk as the patriarchs
Walk according to custom

content: According to 1 John 2:9-11, whoever CLAIMS to be in the light but HATES his brother is what?
explanation: 1 John 2:9-11 — 'Whoever says he is in the light and hates his brother is still in darkness... whoever hates his brother is in the darkness and walks in darkness, and does not know where he is going, because the darkness has blinded his eyes.' Test #2 (after OBEDIENCE 2:3-5) = LOVE for brothers. No love = no Christ.
options: A slow learner (according to the text's framing)
Still under grace (as described in the passage)
Good but weak (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
STILL in DARKNESS — walks in darkness, does NOT know where he goes, because darkness has BLINDED his eyes

content: According to 1 John 2:17, what is the world + its desires? What of those who do God's will?
explanation: 1 John 2:17 — 'And the world is passing away along with its desires, but whoever does the will of God abides forever.' Contrast: WORLD (passing) vs GOD'S WILL (eternal). Same teaching as Jesus 'world passes, my words do NOT' (Mt 24:35). Invest in the ETERNAL = do God's WILL.
options: The world + its desires PASSING AWAY — whoever DOES the will of God ABIDES FOREVER
The world will continue — good deeds rewarded
The world will improve — collaborators rewarded
The world will worsen — survivors rewarded

content: According to 1 John 2:18-19, who appeared showing it's the LAST HOUR? They came FROM where but did NOT belong to whom?
explanation: 1 John 2:18-19 — 'Children, it is the last hour, and as you have heard that antichrist is coming, so now many antichrists have come. Therefore we know that it is the last hour. They went out from us, but they were not of us.' Antichrist is NOT a single person only — many anti-Christ spirits. They left the Church = proof they were NOT truly believers.
options: False prophets — from Israel (as described in the passage)
False kings — from Rome (per the textual narrative)
MANY ANTICHRISTS — they went out FROM us, but they did NOT belong to us
False priests — from temple (according to the text's framing)

content: According to 1 John 3:2, believers are NOW children of God — what is not yet seen? When Christ APPEARS?
explanation: 1 John 3:2 — 'Beloved, we are God's children now, and what we will be has not yet appeared; but we know that when he appears we shall be like him, because we shall see him as he is.' 2 'ALREADY' (children), '+ NOT YET' (what we shall be), 'WILL' (be like him). The LIKENESS (homoioi) to Christ through the SEEING (opsometha).
options: Heaven — will enter (per the textual narrative)
What we WILL BE has not yet appeared — when HE appears we shall be LIKE HIM, for we shall see HIM AS HE IS
Reward — will receive (as described in the passage)
Enemy — will defeat (according to the text's framing)

content: According to 1 John 3:11, what command have believers heard from the beginning?
explanation: 1 John 3:11 — 'For this is the message that you have heard from the beginning, that we should love one another.' 'FROM THE BEGINNING' = since hearing the Gospel. NOT a new command — the same Jesus gave (John 13:34). Next verse (3:12) contrasts: NOT be like Cain (killing brother Abel).
options: Fear God
Keep Moses' law
Give alms
LOVE ONE ANOTHER

content: According to 1 John 3:14, how do believers KNOW they have PASSED from DEATH to LIFE?
explanation: 1 John 3:14 — 'We know that we have passed out of death into life, because we love the brothers. Whoever does not love abides in death.' LOVE = evidence of ETERNAL LIFE. Does NOT say love SAVES — says love is a SIGN of being saved. Unlike Paul (faith = evidence) — John makes LOVE a test.
options: Because we LOVE the brothers — whoever does NOT love abides in DEATH
Because we do miracles (as described in the passage)
Because of wealth (per the textual narrative)
Because of learning (according to the text's framing)

content: According to 1 John 3:15, whoever HATES his brother is what? And does NOT have what?
explanation: 1 John 3:15 — 'Everyone who hates his brother is a murderer, and you know that no murderer has eternal life abiding in him.' Same teaching as Jesus (Mt 5:21-22) — anger toward a brother = like murder. Reason: MURDER begins INSIDE the heart. Next verse (3:16) — Christ LAID DOWN HIS LIFE for us = the MODEL of love.
options: Small sinner (per the textual narrative)
Slow learner (according to the text's framing)
A MURDERER — and NO MURDERER has eternal life abiding in him
Weak in faith (as described in the passage)

content: According to 1 John 3:17, whoever has the WORLD'S GOODS, sees a brother in need, yet CLOSES HIS HEART, what?
explanation: 1 John 3:17 — 'But if anyone has the world's goods and sees his brother in need, yet closes his heart against him, how does God's love abide in him?' 'CLOSES HEART' (kleisē ta splanchna) = shuts the heart's door (splanchna = bowels, seat of compassion). NO God's love if no LOVE shown to needy brothers. Practical love test.
options: Still loved by God
How does God's love abide in him?
Wise saver (per the textual narrative)
Has property rights

content: According to 1 John 4:1, what should believers NOT believe? What must they do?
explanation: 1 John 4:1 — 'Beloved, do not believe every spirit, but test the spirits to see whether they are from God, for many false prophets have gone out into the world.' 'TEST THE SPIRITS' (dokimazete ta pneumata) = examine. Believers should NOT be gullible — must DISCERN true/false. Next verses (4:2-3) give the CRITERION: confessing Jesus Christ has COME in the flesh = from God.
options: Don't believe rumors — verify (per the textual narrative)
Don't believe every teacher — discern (according to the text's framing)
Don't believe every book — read carefully (as described in the passage)
Don't believe every SPIRIT — TEST the spirits whether they are from God, for MANY FALSE PROPHETS have gone out into the world

content: According to 1 John 4:4, whom have believers OVERCOME? Why?
explanation: 1 John 4:4 — 'Little children, you are from God and have overcome them, for he who is in you is greater than he who is in the world.' 'HE IN YOU' = the Holy Spirit / Christ. 'HE IN THE WORLD' = Satan, antichrist. Encouraging believers in spiritual warfare: NO need to fear — Christ HAS WON.
options: Overcome (false) SPIRITS — because HE who is in believers is GREATER than HE who is in the world
Defeated kings — military (per the textual narrative)
Defeated teachers — learning (according to the text's framing)
Defeated enemies — protection (as described in the passage)

content: According to 1 John 4:7, why must believers LOVE ONE ANOTHER?
explanation: 1 John 4:7 — 'Beloved, let us love one another, for love is from God, and whoever loves has been born of God and knows God.' Equation: LOVE = BORN OF GOD = KNOWS GOD. NOT loving = not yet born = does not know God. A short but extremely practical test.
options: Ancestral tradition (as described in the passage)
Mosaic law (per the textual narrative)
Because LOVE is FROM God — whoever LOVES has been BORN OF God and KNOWS God
Society expects (according to the text's framing)

content: According to 1 John 4:9, how was God's love MANIFESTED to us?
explanation: 1 John 4:9 — 'In this the love of God was made manifest among us, that God sent his only Son into the world, so that we might live through him.' 'ONLY SON' (monogenēs) = unique, one-of-a-kind. Same teaching John 3:16. Don't measure God's love by circumstances — measure by the CROSS. Purpose: that we might LIVE (zōē) — not just exist but LIVE eternally.
options: Through creation (according to the text's framing)
God SENT HIS ONLY SON into the world — that we might LIVE through him
Through the OT prophets (as described in the passage)
Through angels (per the textual narrative)

content: According to 1 John 4:20, whoever SAYS 'I love God' but HATES his brother is what?
explanation: 1 John 4:20 — 'If anyone says, "I love God," and hates his brother, he is a liar; for he who does not love his brother whom he has seen cannot love God whom he has not seen.' Argument: brother SEEN = visible test of love. God UNSEEN = harder to verify. Love for God shown through PHYSICAL love.
options: Weak in faith (as described in the passage)
Indifferent (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
Slow learner (according to the text's framing)
A LIAR — for whoever does NOT love his brother whom he HAS SEEN cannot love God whom he HAS NOT SEEN

content: According to 1 John 5:1, whoever BELIEVES Jesus is the CHRIST is what?
explanation: 1 John 5:1 — 'Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ has been born of God, and everyone who loves the Father loves whoever has been born of him.' FAITH produces LOVE. Belonging to the SAME FAMILY (born of the same Father) = naturally LOVES the brothers. Simple principle: accept the Father = love the siblings.
options: BORN OF GOD — and whoever loves the Father loves whoever has been born of him
A Gentile — not yet born (as described in the passage)
Learned — venerable (per the textual narrative)
Wealthy — God-blessed (according to the text's framing)

content: According to 1 John 2:7-8, is the command of love OLD or NEW? Why?
explanation: 1 John 2:7-8 — 'Beloved, I am writing you no new commandment, but an old commandment that you had from the beginning... At the same time, it is a new commandment that I am writing to you, which is true in him and in you, because the darkness is passing away and the true light is already shining.' OLD = from Israel's time (Lev 19:18) or hearing the Gospel. NEW = real in Christ + the Church now.
options: Only old — from OT (according to the text's framing) (as the text indicates)
BOTH OLD (from the beginning) AND NEW (becoming real in Christ + believers) — because DARKNESS is PASSING + the TRUE LIGHT is shining
Only new — never before (as described in the passage) (per the original content)
Expired (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence) (cross-referenced with related passages)

content: According to 1 John 2:20, what do believers have from the Holy One? Therefore they KNOW what?
explanation: 1 John 2:20 — 'But you have been anointed by the Holy One, and you all have knowledge.' 'ANOINTING' (chrisma) = the anointing — from the HOLY ONE (Christ). 'ALL KNOW' does NOT mean knowing every cosmic fact — knowing the essential TRUTH (especially distinguishing TRUE Christ + false). Opposite of GNOSTIC (only a few special).
options: Grace — knows mysteries (according to the text's framing)
Power — knows miracles (per the textual narrative)
Revelation — knows future (as described in the passage)
ANOINTING from the HOLY ONE — and they ALL KNOW

content: According to 1 John 2:27, what does the ANOINTING believers received from Christ do?
explanation: 1 John 2:27 — 'But the anointing that you received from him abides in you, and you have no need that anyone should teach you. But as his anointing teaches you about everything, and is true, and is no lie — just as it has taught you, abide in him.' Does NOT say believers don't need teachers — says they don't need OUTSIDE teachers to DISCERN truth, because of the Spirit. Different from Gnosticism.
options: Gives them miracles (as described in the passage)
Makes them rich (according to the text's framing)
TEACHES them ABOUT EVERYTHING + the anointing is TRUE, not a lie — so abide in him
Makes them fly (per the textual narrative)

content: According to 1 John 3:8, the Son of God appeared to do what to the devil's works?
explanation: 1 John 3:8 — 'Whoever makes a practice of sinning is of the devil, for the devil has been sinning from the beginning. The reason the Son of God appeared was to destroy the works of the devil.' 'DESTROY' (lysē) = dissolve, break apart — like dismantling a structure. Christ does NOT just forgive — he dismantles the devil's SYSTEM. Same teaching as Heb 2:14 (destroy him who had the power of death).
options: DESTROY the WORKS of the devil — for the devil has been SINNING from the beginning
Learn from the devil (according to the text's framing)
Pity the devil (per the textual narrative)
Cooperate with the devil (as described in the passage)

content: According to 1 John 3:12, why did Cain kill his brother Abel? Believers should NOT be like whom?
explanation: 1 John 3:12 — 'We should not be like Cain, who was of the evil one and murdered his brother. And why did he murder him? Because his own deeds were evil and his brother's righteous.' Genesis 4 — Cain killed Abel out of ENVY + EVIL HEART. Note: 1 John says Cain was 'of the evil one' (ek tou ponērou) — NOT physical descent, but spiritual. Gen 3:15 'seed of the serpent'.
options: Property dispute — don't be like greedy kings
Because his WORKS were EVIL + brother's RIGHTEOUS — NOT be like Cain (of the EVIL ONE)
Parents favored brother — don't be like neglected child
Brother took land — don't be like miser (per the textual narrative)

content: According to 1 John 3:23, what is the COMMANDMENT of God (summarized)?
explanation: 1 John 3:23 — 'And this is his commandment, that we believe in the name of his Son Jesus Christ and love one another, just as he has commanded us.' Note: John uses SINGULAR 'commandment' (entolē) — NOT 2 commands but ONE with 2 sides: FAITH + LOVE. Same principle: believe Christ → love brothers.
options: Keep Sabbath + tithe (according to the text's framing)
Pray 5x + fast (per the textual narrative)
Believe prophets + keep law (as described in the passage)
BELIEVE in the NAME of his Son Jesus Christ + LOVE ONE ANOTHER as he commanded

content: According to 1 John 4:2-3, what is the CRITERION to distinguish God's Spirit from antichrist?
explanation: 1 John 4:2-3 — 'By this you know the Spirit of God: every spirit that confesses that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is from God, and every spirit that does not confess Jesus is not from God. This is the spirit of the antichrist.' THE criterion is INCARNATION. Antichrist denies Christ TRULY became flesh. Related to the Docetic heresy.
options: Every spirit that CONFESSES Jesus Christ has COME IN THE FLESH is FROM GOD; every spirit that does NOT confess Jesus is NOT from God — that is the ANTICHRIST spirit, already in the world
Spirit doing miracles is from God (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)
Spirit speaking tongues is from God (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates) (in the context of the passage)
Spirit teaching law is from God (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence) (cross-referenced with related passages)

content: According to 1 John 5:4-5, who overcomes whom (born of God)? What is the VICTORY? Who is the conqueror?
explanation: 1 John 5:4-5 — 'For everyone who has been born of God overcomes the world. And this is the victory that has overcome the world — our faith. Who is it that overcomes the world except the one who believes that Jesus is the Son of God?' 'WORLD' (kosmos) = the anti-God system. FAITH = the conquering weapon. NOT personal effort — but TRUST in Christ as the weapon.
options: Conquer physical enemy — by army — soldiers
Conquer sin — by law-keeping — the obedient
Conquer the WORLD — by FAITH — the one who BELIEVES Jesus is the Son of God
Conquer temptation — by self-control — the persevering

content: According to 1 John 5:6-8, what 3 witnesses (per the older Greek manuscripts: 3 EARTHLY witnesses)?
explanation: 1 John 5:6-8 — 'This is he who came by water and blood — Jesus Christ; not by the water only but by the water and the blood... For there are three that testify: the Spirit and the water and the blood; and these three agree.' 3 WITNESSES: WATER (Christ's baptism in Jordan), BLOOD (Christ's death on the cross), SPIRIT (testimony in believers). Against GNOSTICS (Cerinthus separated Christ from Jesus at the cross).
options: Father + Word + Spirit (as described in the passage)
The SPIRIT + water + blood — 3 witnesses agreeing (about Christ)
3 angels (per the textual narrative)
Paul + Peter + John (according to the text's framing)

content: According to 1 John 5:18-19, what does the one BORN OF GOD do with sin? Where is the WORLD?
explanation: 1 John 5:18-19 — 'We know that everyone who has been born of God does not keep on sinning, but he who was born of God protects him, and the evil one does not touch him. We know that we are from God, and the whole world lies in the power of the evil one.' Does NOT say never sins — says does not LIVE IN sin (1:8 admits we have sin). 'HE WHO WAS BORN OF GOD' = Christ — who protects believers.
options: He does NOT KEEP ON SINNING (sin as lifestyle) — He WHO WAS BORN OF GOD protects him + the EVIL ONE does NOT touch him; the world LIES IN the POWER of the EVIL ONE
Never wrong (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence) (cross-referenced with related passages)
Auto-forgiven (according to the text's framing) (as the text indicates) (in the context of the passage)
No need to repent (as described in the passage) (per the original content) (per the textual narrative)
tags: Born of God: evil cannot touch

content: According to 1 John 5:20-21, what did the Son of God come to GIVE? What is the closing warning?
explanation: 1 John 5:20-21 — 'And we know that the Son of God has come and has given us understanding, so that we may know him who is true; and we are in him who is true, in his Son Jesus Christ. He is the true God and eternal life. Little children, keep yourselves from idols.' 'TRUE' (alēthinos) repeats 3x. 'JESUS IS GOD' (explicit). IDOLS = anything replacing Christ.
options: Power — use it well (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)
Wealth — manage it (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
UNDERSTANDING to KNOW the TRUE ONE (Jesus Christ is the true God + eternal life) — closing: KEEP YOURSELVES from IDOLS
Peace — pass it on (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)
